item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what the fuck

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the fact that Wells is clearly a madman, I can absolutely guarantee that I have met religious fanatics twice as insane in real life on all sides of matters.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is the name of the program?

It's written as "Late Nights with Avery Dean" throughout the piece except for the Description, which reads "After Hours with Avery Dean."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ah, this one's just classic.

I think it could stand to be a bit "skippier", but I like it just fine as-is. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait so how were the figure's eyes red if the tape was in black and white

item.title: Re: Love the dialogue
item.content:encoded:
I'm also confused / curious about why the audience rushes the stage when the shirt came off to reveal the sigil.

It makes a huge difference in interpretation between my speculations of:

1. some kind of paranormal compulsion to close in the distance to be easier targets for the loop of fire
2. some kind of paranormal compulsion from the "opposite side" to tackle/restrain the guy before he ignites
3. just plain old fashioned "mob justice" mentality that had nothing paranormal about it at all. But I don't like this one for being boring. Unfortunately the real world is just plain boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious to know what SCP-343 would say about this…event…

Good stuff. Certainly well written. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs can be anything, really: events, people, objects. For example, SCP-186 is simply a historical battle that the Foundation covered up. The log of extranormal events is meant for far briefer descriptions than a typical SCP article is, so if a writer wants to delve into their extranormal event for more than a paragraph’s worth of text, it’s totally reasonable to commandeer an SCP slot for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded, It's a good read but I don't think it really needs to be an SCP article. I can see the transcript holding up on its own as a tale.

item.title: Love the dialogue
item.content:encoded:
The dialogue captures the repetition, hesitation, and chat show speech patterns so well. I found it really believable.

Only criticisms are that I'm not clear on why the audience start running towards the stage at first - and in the description people are said to have "disappeared", but the transcript implies that they've not disappeared, they've been burned to death.

I do like the imagery of the cameras continuing to roll for another 16 minutes in the silent aftermath until they too are consumed.

Technical / historical question - when recording for TV in 1966, would they have been recording to film or tape in the cameras, or transmitting a video feed elsewhere for remote recording or transmission? It only makes a difference because if it's the former, the camera film or tape would be consumed in the fire, and so no recording would exist for there to be a transcript of.

item.title: Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
The header image is consisted of following two images from Wikimedia Commons, both of which are public domain.

  • Wells: "File:Jack Barry NYWTS.jpg"
  • Dean: "File:Eddie cantor television 1952.JPG"

Love how the interview starts with a friendly dialogue than slowly going upwards using uncomfortable questions until it reaches its highest point where the horror happens. It has been nailed very well.

I love this so much toxic religious stuff is my thing aaahhhhhhh

Good job! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one stewed with me for a few hours. I hope my ultimate reason for upvoting isn't interpreted as denigrating; your work is an inspiration, kaktus. I wasn't really touched by this one because… I don't know, for the subject matter and message it felt hollow, or too easy. But it's still a very worthy message to convey. I've given it a +1 just for that.

I do feel like the idea itself, as an SCP, is good, but the execution is unclear in its details, and it's muddied by what's left unsaid. Sometimes that's great, but in this case I feel like being overly blunt isn't so bad when you've taken the step of literally damning someone to hell (deservedly). For the message itself, in any form, this should succeed. Too few people think about what has been done to too many in such clothing.

Again, I love what you write and hope this isn't construed as too negative :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really gotta love articles that emphasize human horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me giving wells his daily perscription of chill pills

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am in the same boat. It feels like either the figure or the phenomenon is the SCP (in the latter case its singular nature seems off and in the former, it's definitely not verifiably neutralized, or if it is I missed how).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://imgur.com/a/EjvwkW6

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: damn wells take a chill pill

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a loaded question, honestly curious - I'm confused why this wouldn't be an extranormal event file rather than an SCP. Is the film or recording itself doing something I missed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just want to prop this feedback up as a pretty good summation of my own. Really neat piece, thanks for sharing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is the kind of story that actually made me feel a lump in my throat. well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn good stuff. Setting the skip up with this formal, controlled setting and gradually demolishing it and revealing the whole facade was very well done; it ends up somewhere completely different to where it started and feels earned. And ofc anything exploring that sweet sweet intersection of colonialism and religion does it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The characterization of Wells and the conversational descent into that patronizing kind of racism was really well done. Like Tanhony mentions, it does a good job showing us that we've got an incomplete image while not overplaying its hand.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
High praise, Tanhony.

I was so sure this was a made-up movie name.

He had a few films in the right time-frame that I could used, but I'll be fucked before I don't reference A Thousand Clown in an article.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this.

+ Tanhony rambles
- TURN IT OFF

I'm a big fan of media-based skips, and the time period you chose to set it in lends a really nice atmosphere to the interview too. I can really imagine this cheesy, hokey interview slowly turning uncomfortable and then horrifying as Wells rants on and on. Something that I found especially interesting is the fact that the interview itself is designated as the SCP, not because it itself is anomalous, but because it exists as the only evidence of this anomaly. That's a nice touch - it really emphasizes how the Foundation sees even this second-hand source as a threat to normalcy.

The appearance of the shadow entity and what happens to Wells at the end, along with what's said during the interview, along with the way he talks about his life and what happened in Borneo, hints at this bigger story that we don't quite see the entirety of - there's a great sense of mystery there without things feeling too deliberately obfuscated.

Something that sort of bugs me is we don't get much sense of the aftermath here - a lot of people disappeared, and some real shit went down, but we stop before we see how people respond to that. That's not necessarily bad, though, as it does a good job of leaving you wanting more, but I just thought I'd comment on it.

Also, just a really nitpicky thing:

However, when Robards had to cancel due to illness show producer Jack Coffman contacted American evangelical Christian missionary Reverend William "Willy" Wells to come on the show instead.

This sentence feels like it kind of runs on a bit. I feel like an additional comma after 'illness' here would help with that, maybe.

Jason Robards, who was in the middle of a press tour for the film A Thousand Clowns.

I was so sure this was a made-up movie name. It's nice to know that there's so much beauty in our real human earth history.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fire (hyuck) imagery and religious tones. Dialogue felt melodramatic but in a good way — the piece benefitted from it. As good now as it was in chat.

item.title: And he went down, down, down...
item.content:encoded:
Hey all, djkaktus here again. Was hit with some spontoneity the other day and decided to write about something that used to scare me when I was a kid.

Thanks to aismallard and rounderhouse for reading through this one in chat, I hope you enjoy it!
